# Security Checklist: GitHub Pages Build-And-Deploy Pipeline

**Purpose**: Validate security requirements for Actions permissions, token and secret boundaries, repository administration scope, staging indexing protection, and the DOC-012 public launch gate before implementation.
**Created**: 2026-06-23
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Least-Privilege Actions Permissions

- [x] CHK001 Are Pages workflow permissions limited to source read access, Pages publication access, and OIDC token access needed by standard Pages deployment? [Completeness, Spec §FR-002, Plan §Phase 1 Design Summary]
- [x] CHK002 Is the allowed permission set specific enough to reject broader `contents`, `actions`, `deployments`, `pull-requests`, or `packages` write scopes? [Clarity, Spec §FR-002, Contract §Required Permissions]
- [x] CHK003 Are build/upload and deploy requirements tied to standard Pages deployment rather than repository administration or release automation privileges? [Consistency, Spec §FR-010, Spec §FR-016]
- [x] CHK004 Does the spec explicitly prohibit repository secrets, deploy keys, personal access tokens, or other secret-backed token inputs for DOC-011 Pages deployment? [Completeness, Security, Spec §FR-019]

## Repository Administration Boundaries

- [x] CHK005 Are requirements clear that repository Pages settings remain a manual maintainer setup step, not an automated CLI/API mutation? [Completeness, Spec §FR-016, Spec §Staging Visibility And Operator Documentation]
- [x] CHK006 Are runbook requirements specific enough to prevent future agents from presenting `gh api`, REST, or GraphQL repository-setting mutations as part of DOC-011? [Clarity, Spec §FR-016]
- [x] CHK007 Are manual setup requirements consistent with the plan's KISS decision to avoid custom deploy scripts and administrative automation? [Consistency, Plan §Constitution Check, Plan §Phase 1 Design Summary]

## Staging Indexing Protection

- [x] CHK008 Are crawler-disallow and noindex/nofollow requirements both documented, with distinct roles for `robots.txt` and page-level meta robots guidance? [Completeness, Spec §FR-011, Spec §FR-012, Spec §Staging Visibility And Operator Documentation]
- [x] CHK009 Is the exact `robots.txt` content specified narrowly enough to avoid accidentally allowing crawler access before DOC-012? [Clarity, Spec §Staging Visibility And Operator Documentation]
- [x] CHK010 Is the noindex/nofollow guard specified as a single global Starlight `head` entry so implementation cannot scatter inconsistent page-level guards? [Clarity, Spec §Staging Visibility And Operator Documentation]
- [x] CHK011 Are the staging preview requirements consistent with the security goal of direct reviewer access without public indexing or crawler discovery? [Consistency, Spec §User Story 3]

## DOC-012 Launch Gate

- [x] CHK012 Are requirements explicit that DOC-012 is the only owner for removing indexing protection, changing the custom domain, or changing base-path launch assumptions? [Completeness, Spec §FR-013, Spec §FR-017]
- [x] CHK013 Are PR review packet requirements specific enough to force deferred public launch work to name DOC-012 rather than silently expanding DOC-011? [Traceability, Spec §PR Review Packet Requirements]
- [x] CHK014 Are roadmap, spec, and plan statements consistent that DOC-011 is staging-only while DOC-012 remains the public launch gate? [Consistency, Roadmap §DOC-011, Roadmap §DOC-012, Plan §Phase 1 Design Summary]

## Notes

- Focus areas selected: least-privilege Actions permissions, no repository admin mutation through CLI/API, no secrets or broad token permissions, staging noindex/nofollow plus robots disallow guard, and preserving DOC-012 as the only public launch gate.
- Depth level: Standard security requirements quality review.
- Actor/timing: Reviewer before DOC-011 implementation tasks.

## Verification Re-run

- [x] CHK015 Are credential-boundary requirements now explicit enough to prohibit repository or organization secrets, deploy keys, personal access tokens, GitHub App tokens, custom token inputs, and long-lived deploy credentials? [Completeness, Spec §FR-019]
- [x] CHK016 Are token requirements consistent between the spec, plan, workflow contract, data model, and quickstart? [Consistency, Spec §Credential And Token Boundaries]
- [x] CHK017 Are broad write permissions explicitly excluded while preserving the documented standard Pages permission set? [Clarity, Spec §Credential And Token Boundaries, Contract §Required Permissions]
- [x] CHK018 Are repository administration boundaries still clear that Pages settings are manual and not automated through CLI/API? [Consistency, Spec §FR-016]
- [x] CHK019 Are staging indexing protection requirements still tied to DOC-012 as the only launch guard removal owner? [Consistency, Spec §FR-013, Plan §Phase 1 Design Summary]
